claim_boundary: The ecosystem audit documents optional composition and public evidence roles; it does not assert that every external repository is installed, API-compatible, licensed for redistribution, or independently validated for this study.

classification_rubric.fork_rule: Forks may be selected only as bounded source, method, conformance, or workflow references; they may not be core integrations or required runtime dependencies.
classification_rubric.promotion_rule: A repository may be promoted to core only after a typed interface, deterministic project oracle, issue draft, tests, and compatibility boundary exist.
classification_rubric.purpose: Make inclusion decisions reviewable. Inclusion means a bounded role in the wider research ecosystem; it never means an automatic import, clone, runtime dependency, endorsement, or redistribution permission.

core.definition: Directly reusable capability with a typed local adapter, issue track, and verification oracle.

excluded.definition: Insufficient project relevance, superseded implementation, restricted-data orientation, or generic infrastructure.

reference.definition: Contextual repository retained in the complete audit but not active in a pipeline.

strong.definition: Materially useful data, method, policy, publication, or harness component used through a bounded interface.

supporting.definition: Useful workflow pattern, optional publication target, agent skill, or comparative reference.

external_tools.purpose: prepare or manage optional OIA work

pipelines.gate: licence, source date, evidence grade, and redistribution status required
pipelines.purpose: Build the national public-source service census and evidence graph

pipelines.gate: human-reviewed oracle and non-compensatory clinical constraints
pipelines.purpose: Represent funding, eligibility, safety, and policy assumptions as executable but reviewable contracts

pipelines.gate: reference-oracle parity, deterministic seeds, DSA/PSA/VOI and claim-boundary checks
pipelines.purpose: Run access, cost, equity, optimisation, adoption, capacity, uncertainty, and research-prioritisation analyses

pipelines.gate: agents may propose; deterministic tools verify; human review controls publication
pipelines.purpose: Maintain an auditable, machine-readable, multi-agent development and research workflow

pipelines.gate: dry-run plans by default; checksums and publication-readiness gate before writes
pipelines.purpose: Create reproducible deposits, manuscripts, diagrams, public dashboards, and archival mirrors

pipelines.gate: no request submission without explicit human action
pipelines.purpose: Use optional OIA requests to improve public evidence without making them a model dependency

pipelines.gate: dashboard and social outputs generated only from frozen aggregate artefacts
pipelines.purpose: Surface aggregate outputs and communicate limitations without creating a second source of truth

pipelines.gate: not a factual source, not an autonomous policy decision-maker, and not required for reproduction
pipelines.purpose: Support private local drafting and agent workflows while keeping evidence and decisions deterministic

limitations: Private, organisation-only, deleted, disabled, or otherwise non-public repositories are outside scope.
A public repository description is not proof of API stability, correctness, maintenance, security, or licence compatibility.
Suitability is a project-specific engineering judgement recorded separately from repository metadata.
The frozen profile order or fork state may change after the snapshot; scheduled checks report metadata drift only.
Fork classification follows the GitHub public fork filter at snapshot time and does not imply endorsement of upstream code.

method.future_refresh: GitHub REST metadata drift job; refresh does not automatically alter suitability decisions.
method.primary: Manual and machine-assisted review of all profile repository pages in last-updated order.
